app.name: Droidsmith
app.description: Inspect devices, stage actions, and keep every risky Android operation traceable.

runtime.notAttachedHint: Device IPC is available when the app is launched with npm run tauri:dev.
runtime.notAttachedPrefix: Device IPC is available when the app is launched with

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics center
diagnostics.description: Preview a privacy-filtered snapshot of Droidsmith, ADB health, connected-device aliases, recent failed operations, and crash logs before saving it.
diagnostics.noUploadBody: The bundle is generated on this computer. Raw serials, network device addresses, pairing secrets, credential-like values, and host paths are removed by the Rust backend before preview or save.
diagnostics.saveFailed: Could not save the support bundle: {{message}}
diagnostics.wipeBody: This deletes rotating crash logs and host-wide recovery records. Per-device action journals and install audit records are preserved because they power audit and recovery. Saved bundles outside Droidsmith are not deleted.
diagnostics.wiped: Removed {{count}} diagnostic file(s) ({{size}}). Action and install audit journals were preserved.

bugreport.title: Android bugreport
bugreport.description: Capture a device-level Android diagnostic ZIP for failures that need more context than Droidsmith's redacted host support bundle.
bugreport.desktopRequiredBody: Bugreport capture requires the Droidsmith desktop shell, an authorized Android device, and ADB.
bugreport.noAuthorizedBody: Connect and authorize an Android device before capturing a bugreport.
bugreport.warningTitle: This report can contain private and security-sensitive data
bugreport.warningBody: Android bugreports can include dumpsys and dumpstate output, Logcat history, screenshots, account and network identifiers, app activity, device state, and values that may be personal data or secrets.
bugreport.privacyContract: Droidsmith treats the ZIP as opaque: it validates only archive structure, writes a redacted metadata sidecar, and does not inspect for redaction, upload, attach, or open the report.
bugreport.acknowledge: I understand the likely contents and want to save this sensitive report locally.
bugreport.running: Android is building the bugreport. This can take several minutes.
bugreport.selecting: Choose a local .zip destination in the native save dialog.
bugreport.savedContract: Neither file was opened, attached, or uploaded. The metadata sidecar identifies the report with its size and SHA-256 without copying device or build identifiers.

devices.title: Devices
devices.description: Scan USB and TCP/IP targets, confirm ADB readiness, and see enough context to choose the right device before any action runs.
devices.transportTrustBody: {{transport}} does not provide the authenticated pairing guarantees of Android Wireless Debugging. Traffic and device data may be exposed to others on this network.
devices.transportTrustAcknowledge: Allow privileged operations over this connection until I select another device.
devices.selectHint: Select a device to view its dashboard. Review state and transport before launching any action.
devices.authorizeOneBody: Device {{serial}} is connected but has not been authorized yet.
devices.authorizeManyBody: The following devices are connected but have not been authorized yet:
devices.step1: Look at the device screen for the Allow USB debugging? dialog.
devices.step2: Optionally check Always allow from this computer to skip this next time.
devices.step3: Tap Allow, then click Refresh below.
devices.launchDesktopTitle: Launch the desktop shell to scan hardware
devices.launchDesktopBodyPrefix: This browser preview can render the interface, but device IPC only exists inside Tauri. Start the desktop shell with
devices.launchDesktopBodySuffix: when you want Droidsmith to talk to ADB.
devices.noAdbBodyMiddle: Android Studio defaults, Homebrew, and common Linux package-manager locations. Install Android platform tools or run
devices.noDevicesStep1: Connect USB and enable developer options.
devices.noDevicesStep3: Or use the Wireless tab for Wi-Fi pairing.
devices.linuxPermsBody: One or more devices report no permissions. Avoid world-writable 0666 rules; use the distribution's device-access group and maintained Android udev rules.
devices.linuxPermsDoctor: Run the connection doctor below to check group membership and udev-rule coverage, then follow its official setup link.
devices.mustAuthorize: Device must be authorized before it can be selected
devices.reconnectUsb: Reconnect the USB cable and wait a few seconds.
devices.fileTransferMode: Ensure USB mode is set to File Transfer / MTP; some devices block debugging in charge-only mode.

health.title: ADB lifecycle health
health.probing: Probing the ADB server and discovery backend...
health.cancelling: Cancelling and waiting for the current ADB process to exit...
health.recoveryBody: This host-wide action restarts the local ADB server, then asks only offline transports to reconnect. The exact sequence is shown below and a durable operation record is written before the first command runs.
health.recoveryWarning: All connected tools that share this ADB server may briefly disconnect. No device data is changed, but active transfers and shell sessions can be interrupted.
health.copyFallback: Clipboard access was unavailable. Select and copy the diagnostics text manually.

recoveryOutcome.succeeded: ADB restarted and the offline reconnect request completed.
recoveryOutcome.failed: Recovery stopped after a failed step. Review the diagnostics before retrying.
recoveryOutcome.cancelled: Recovery was cancelled and recorded. Review device state before retrying.

controls.screenshotBody: Capture the current screen to a local PNG file.
controls.processManagerBody: Snapshot of running processes sorted by memory usage.
controls.noProcessesBody: Load a process snapshot to inspect memory-heavy apps.
controls.noMatchingProcessesBody: Clear the filter or search for another process name.
controls.fileManagerBody: Browse, pull, and inspect files on the device.
controls.noDirectoryBodySuffix: to inspect device files and pull local copies.
controls.emptyDirectoryBody: This path has no visible entries.
controls.connectionsReadFailed: Couldn't read connections: {{message}}. Some devices restrict ss/netstat without root.
controls.noConnectionsBody: Load active sockets to review endpoints and owning processes.
controls.noMatchingConnectionsBody: Clear the filter or search for another address, process, or state.

wireless.title: Wireless
wireless.description: Pair Android 11+ devices over Wi-Fi, discover mDNS pairing/connect endpoints, and attach paired devices without leaving Droidsmith.
wireless.name: Name
wireless.legacyTcpEndpoint: This endpoint uses legacy adb tcpip and is not authenticated. Do not select this for an Android Wireless Debugging pairing endpoint.
wireless.desktopRequiredBody: Wireless ADB commands run inside the Tauri runtime.
wireless.noAdbBody: Install Android platform tools or stage the bundled sidecar.
wireless.noServicesBody: mDNS did not return pairing or connect targets on this network.
wireless.discoveredServicesBody: Pairing services use the QR code above; connect services attach already-paired devices.
wireless.vpnHintTitle: An active VPN or tunnel may be blocking the device route
wireless.vpnHintBody: Droidsmith found an active tunnel interface after the wireless ADB command failed.
wireless.vpnHintStep1: Temporarily pause the VPN or allow local-network traffic, then retry.
wireless.vpnHintStep2: Confirm the computer and Android device can reach each other on the same trusted Wi-Fi network.
wireless.mdnsHintTitle: mDNS name resolution may be unavailable
wireless.mdnsHintBody: The local service name could not be resolved and ADB's mDNS health check did not succeed.
wireless.mdnsHintStep1: Rescan mDNS services after confirming Wireless debugging is still enabled on Android.
wireless.mdnsHintStep2: If discovery remains unavailable, enter the IP address and port shown by Android instead of the .local name.
wireless.failureDiagnosticsPrivacy: This report excludes the endpoint, adapter name, device serial, network address, and VPN product name.

apps.title: Apps
apps.description: Review installed packages with precise filters, and a preview-before-apply workflow for disable, enable, uninstall, and data-clear actions.
apps.installCancelling: Cancelling install and abandoning its session…
apps.installSucceededBody: Installed {{count}} package part(s), {{size}} total. The package list has been refreshed.
apps.installNoAutomaticOverride: Droidsmith did not apply a retry override automatically.
apps.installDowngradeWarning: This retries with -d and may replace a newer installed build with an older one. App data can become incompatible or unusable.
apps.installLowTargetWarning: This retries with --bypass-low-target-sdk-block. The app targets obsolete Android protections and can expose the device or its data to known platform risks.
apps.installOverrideAudit: Only this retry receives the override. The exact flag and this explicit confirmation are written to the local install audit journal.
apps.installGrantExpired: The selected package authorization expired. Choose the package again before retrying.
apps.installedPackagesBody: Actions preview before applying. Disable and enable are reversible via the journal.
apps.apkExportRunningBody: Pulling and validating {{count}} base/split APK files, then writing a manifest.
apps.apkExportSaved: Exported and hashed {{count}} base/split APK files in {{file}}.
apps.legacyEntriesDetected: Structurally valid app-data entries were detected in {{file}}. Restore compatibility and completeness are not verified.
apps.legacyNoDataBody: The structurally valid archive in {{file}} contains no recognized app-data entries. Do not treat it as a data backup.
apps.legacyLimitations: Advanced only: deprecated adb backup varies by Android and OEM, requires device confirmation, and cannot prove a future restore. Droidsmith validates the archive and records evidence; it does not call adb restore or claim recoverability.
apps.recoveryExportedReady: Baseline exported. This reviewed action has not run yet.
apps.recoveryInspecting: Comparing baseline with the live device...
apps.recoveryReviewBody: Exported {{date}} for Android user {{user}}. Review every row before applying; import itself changed nothing.
apps.desktopRequiredBody: Package management runs inside the Tauri runtime.
apps.noAuthorizedBody: Connect a device and accept the USB debugging prompt, then refresh.
apps.noMatchingPackagesBody: Adjust the search text or switch package filters to bring the list back.
apps.noPermissionsFoundBody: This package did not report runtime or install permissions.
apps.permissionToggleFailed: Couldn't change this permission: {{message}}. Some permissions are fixed by the system or app policy and can't be toggled here.
apps.journalBody: Recent package actions for this device. Disable and enable entries can be undone once.
apps.journalEmptyBody: Apply a package action and Droidsmith will record it here.

debloat.title: Debloat
debloat.description: Choose an OEM-aware pack, understand the risk before applying it, and keep every change reversible through the journal.
debloat.choosePackBody: Each pack targets a specific OEM or ROM. Packages are grouped by removal safety tier.
debloat.confirmApplyBody: Droidsmith will disable the selected packages from {{name}} for this Android user.
debloat.unsafeSelectedBody: These entries can break core device or OEM features. Verify every package ID before continuing.
debloat.confirmJournal: Each attempted change and its verified result will be written to the per-device journal for review and supported undo.
debloat.desktopRequiredBody: Debloat operations run inside the Tauri runtime.
debloat.noAuthorizedBody: Connect a device and accept the USB debugging prompt.
debloat.packErrorsBody: These bundled pack files were skipped. Fix or remove them; healthy packs below still work.
debloat.compatibilityOverride: I reviewed the mismatched or unknown device checks and accept applying this exact pack revision anyway. This override will be recorded in the action journal.
debloat.planFailed: Pack plan could not be created
debloat.entryUnavailableForPlan: This package is not available for the selected device and Android user.
debloat.journalUndo: All changes are recorded in the per-device journal and can be undone from the Apps tab.
debloat.cancelledBeforeApply: Cancelled before this package was applied.
debloat.cancelledSummary: Cancellation stopped the queue after the current package finished.

verify.missing: Package disappeared after apply; expected it to remain installed and disabled.
verify.still_enabled: Package is still enabled after apply.
verify.unknown: Package state could not be verified after apply.

mirror.title: Mirror
mirror.description: Launch scrcpy sessions to mirror and control Android devices. Requires scrcpy installed on your system.
mirror.recordDestinationHint: When you launch, a native save dialog will choose the .mp4 or .mkv recording destination. Presets never retain host paths.
mirror.desktopRequiredBody: Mirror sessions run inside the Tauri runtime.
mirror.scrcpyInstallPrefix: Droidsmith uses scrcpy for device mirroring. Install it from
mirror.noAuthorizedBody: Connect and authorize a device to start mirroring.
mirror.probingCapabilitiesBody: Droidsmith is reading the scrcpy version and the selected device's reported video encoders before enabling launch.
mirror.sessionRunningBody: scrcpy is running as PID {{pid}}. Close the scrcpy window or stop it here to end the session.

user_stopped.title: Mirror session stopped
user_stopped.body: The session was stopped from Droidsmith.

unsupported_option.title: Installed scrcpy rejected an option
unsupported_option.body: Recheck capabilities. The scrcpy binary may have changed or may not support a saved option.

device_disconnected.title: Device disconnected during mirroring
device_disconnected.body: Reconnect and authorize the same device, then start a new session.

encoder_failed.title: Device video encoder failed
encoder_failed.body: Choose another reported encoder or H.264, reduce the maximum size, and retry.

permission_denied.title: Android denied scrcpy control
permission_denied.body: Enable the OEM's additional USB debugging security setting or use the SDK keyboard mode.

adb_failed.title: ADB could not start the mirror session
adb_failed.body: Review device authorization and ADB health, then retry after the transport is stable.

signaled.title: scrcpy was terminated externally
signaled.body: Another process or the operating system ended scrcpy without an exit code.

process_exited.title: scrcpy exited with an error
process_exited.body: Review the bounded diagnostics below for the exact host or device failure.

console.title: Console
console.description: Run read-only ADB shell commands directly; review and journal every mutation before it executes.
console.hint: Type a command below. Arrow keys recall history.
console.desktopRequiredBody: Shell commands run inside the Tauri runtime.
console.noAuthorizedBody: Connect and authorize a device to use the console.
console.reviewMutationBody: This command can change device state. Verify the exact argv and target before running it; the intent, outcome, and redacted output will be written to the device journal.

logcat.title: Logcat
logcat.description: Stream device logs incrementally with bounded history, reconnect markers, filters, and export.
logcat.desktopRequiredBody: Logcat runs inside the Tauri runtime.
logcat.noAuthorizedBody: Connect and authorize a device to view logs.
logcat.startHint: Press Start tail to begin capturing logs.

fastboot.title: Fastboot
fastboot.description: Inspect bootloader-mode devices, partition state, slots, and safety-critical variables before any destructive operation.
fastboot.safetyWarning: Fastboot operations can be destructive. Droidsmith intentionally does not expose flash, erase, or lock/unlock commands through the GUI. Use the Console tab with
fastboot.installSuffix: binary. Install it from the Android SDK or run the platform-tools fetch script.
fastboot.noBootloaderDevices: No devices in bootloader/fastboot mode. Reboot a device into the bootloader with
fastboot.queryHint: Press Query vars to read device information.
fastboot.queryAllFailed: Couldn't read any fastboot variables. The device may have left fastboot mode, or the bootloader rejected the query. Reconnect and try again.
fastboot.queryNoValues: The query completed but the device reported no values for these variables.

hostDoctor.title: Host connection doctor
hostDoctor.description: Check Platform Tools, ADB server configuration, anonymized transport states, and bounded Windows/Linux USB prerequisites without changing the host or device.
hostDoctor.idle: No scan has run. The doctor does not install drivers, edit rules, restart ADB, or retain serials and keys.

adb_unrunnable.title: ADB was found but could not enumerate transports

adb_version_unknown.title: ADB version could not be verified

platform_tools_blocked.title: This Platform Tools release is known-bad

unauthorized.title: Android is waiting for USB debugging authorization

no_permissions.title: Linux cannot open the Android USB device

usb_visible_adb_missing.title: Android USB hardware is visible but absent from ADB

no_usb_transport.title: No Android USB transport was detected

windows_driver_missing.title: Windows has no recognized ADB interface

linux_access_incomplete.title: Linux Android USB access is incomplete

server_config_override.title: Custom ADB server configuration is active

client_server_mismatch.title: ADB client and server versions differ

developerOptions.title: Enable Developer Options

content.0: Open Settings on your Android device.
content.1: Scroll to About phone or About tablet.
content.2: Tap Build number 7 times. You will see a toast saying you are now a developer.
content.3: Go back to Settings; Developer options is now visible.

developerOptions.tip: On Samsung: Settings > About phone > Software information > Build number.

usbDebugging.title: Enable USB Debugging

content.1: Scroll down to USB debugging and toggle it on.

usbDebugging.tip: On Xiaomi/MIUI, also enable USB debugging security settings to allow app installs.

drivers.title: Install USB Drivers (Windows)

content.0: Most devices work with the Google USB Driver from the Android SDK Manager.
content.1: Samsung devices use the Samsung USB Driver from samsung.com.
content.2: Other OEMs list ADB or USB drivers on their support pages.
content.3: macOS and Linux usually do not need extra drivers.

drivers.tip: On Linux, you may need a udev rule; Droidsmith shows the fix automatically when this happens.

authorize.title: Connect and Authorize

content.0: Connect your device via USB cable.
content.1: Your device will show an Allow USB debugging? dialog.
content.2: Check Always allow from this computer if this is your personal machine.
content.3: Tap Allow. The device should appear in Droidsmith's Devices tab.

authorize.tip: If the dialog does not appear, try a different USB cable; charge-only cables do not support data.

wireless.title: Wireless Debugging (Android 11+)

content.0: Open Settings > Developer options > Wireless debugging.
content.2: In Droidsmith, go to the Wireless tab.
content.3: Use QR pairing: scan the QR code shown in Droidsmith with your device.
content.4: Or use manual pairing: enter the host, port, and 6-digit code from your device.

wireless.tip: Both devices must be on the same Wi-Fi network. The pairing code changes each time.
